global_context.scene_description: A cinematic editorial overhead flat-lay shot on a dark walnut wood surface. Hero subject: a messy overlapping pile of approximately 7-8 HVAC service repair invoices/receipts, each clearly legible with visible repair charges and dates, fanned and stacked like a poker spread. The invoices are from various generic HVAC companies (no specific brand names) and show repair charges visible to the viewer: '$487 - Capacitor replacement - June 2024', '$612 - Refrigerant recharge - July 2024', '$390 - Fan motor - Aug 2024', '$850 - Compressor diagnostic - May 2025', '$235 - Service call - June 2025', '$1,100 - Coil cleaning + repair - July 2025', '$520 - Emergency dispatch - Aug 2025'. The repair invoices look worn, slightly crumpled at edges, some marked with red pen or check marks. Sitting cleanly ON TOP of the messy pile is a single crisp ASH new install quote, fresh white paper, with bold clean typography reading: 'ASH COOLING & HEATING' header, 'NEW AC INSTALL', '$9,500', '0% APR FINANCING', 'PRICE MATCH GUARANTEE'. The contrast between the messy pile of small repair receipts and the single clean ASH quote on top is the entire mechanism. Single soft theatrical top-light catches the documents, dramatic shadow falloff to near-black walnut grain at edges. Small headline overlaid at top in bold uppercase white sans-serif: 'YOU HAVE SPENT MORE PUTTING IT OFF.' Small italic caption at bottom: 'New AC install. $9,500. 0% APR. Price match. Skip the next repair.' Mood: cold mathematical reveal of opportunity cost. Editorial document still life, NOT documentary photo, NOT cartoon.
